title: AW generation and checks consume command-generation APIs

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Convert AW generation and checking scripts into host wrappers around command_generation public APIs while keeping AW-specific runtime boundary policy in AW.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep command-generation as a maintainer dependency only; AW remains the owner of AW command meaning, generated outputs, and runtime-boundary policy.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Implementation order, helper names, and validation tightening inside this lane.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The lane needs command semantic drift, runtime dependency drift, or files outside the declared owner surfaces.
canonical_core.next_action: Replace internal command-generation imports or duplicated generic logic in AW wrappers with package APIs and add guard tests.
completion_criteria: AW generation uses package public APIs.
AW checks call package APIs for generic render/freshness/conformance work.
AW-specific runtime boundary and shipped dependency checks remain in AW.
A future external command-generation dependency can replace the internal package without substantial wrapper changes.

goal: Convert AW generation and checking scripts into host wrappers around command_generation public APIs while keeping AW-specific runtime boundary policy in AW.
non_goals: Do not move AW-specific policy checks into command-generation.
Do not change generated output layout.

intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: no, it completes one child lane

execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run pytest tests/test_command_generation_artifacts.py tests/test_command_generation_primitive_executor.py -q; uv --directory ../command-generation run pytest -q; uv run pytest tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py -q
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing command semantics, generated package layout, runtime dependency policy, or unrelated package internals.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated AW runtime behavior, unrelated package internals, or generated outputs not covered by freshness proof.

post_decomposition_delegation.reason: The current branch spans two repos and active PR context; hand off only after a lane becomes isolated and mechanical.

adaptive_assurance.reason: The epic changes ownership and dependency boundaries across AW and command-generation.

active_milestone.scope: Convert AW generation and checking scripts into host wrappers around command_generation public APIs while keeping AW-specific runtime boundary policy in AW.
active_milestone.ready: ready after dependency lanes complete: command-generation-public-api-host-manifest, command-generation-non-aw-fixture, command-generation-renderer-decoupling

immediate_next_action: Replace internal command-generation imports or duplicated generic logic in AW wrappers with package APIs and add guard tests.
blockers: None currently known; dependency order is recorded in the decomposition.
invariants: Do not add command-generation to AW runtime dependencies.
Do not hide AW-specific behavior inside generic command_generation renderers.
Generated package proof remains the freshness and target-proof authority.
completion_criteria: AW generation uses package public APIs.
AW checks call package APIs for generic render/freshness/conformance work.
AW-specific runtime boundary and shipped dependency checks remain in AW.
A future external command-generation dependency can replace the internal package without substantial wrapper changes.
proof_expectations: AW wrapper tests prevent internal renderer imports.
Generated-output freshness and package proof pass through external package APIs.

execution_run.what happened: planning scaffold tightened before implementation continuation
execution_run.result for continuation: execute only when dependencies are satisfied
execution_run.next step: Replace internal command-generation imports or duplicated generic logic in AW wrappers with package APIs and add guard tests.

proof_report.evidence for proof achieved state: Registered execplan plus parent decomposition reference.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: No lane is closed by planning alone; implementation proof must satisfy the issue criteria.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Any acceptance criterion is missing, validation fails, or command-generation becomes an AW runtime dependency.

signals found: The agent started direct implementation of an eight-issue epic before promoting the roadmap candidates into decomposition and execplan surfaces.
signals routed: GitHub #1215 tracks the epic candidate-lane promotion guardrail discovered during dogfooding.
